sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Info action to introduce basic ideas for reforming Cardano treasury governance into public discussion and encourage ecosystem collaboration on improved treasury governance processes.
Stated amount: not stated in document

Deliverables: Discuss reforming treasury governance to provide clearer strategic direction and guardrails., Consider amendments or refinements to current governance processes and the constitution., Consider a treasury governance process involving votes on the NCL, a budget, and an overall treasury withdrawal., Consider a budget structure across eight domains including core protocol, tooling, research, commercial growth, operations, community, other public goods, and reserve funding.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Cardano treasury governance is currently in a poor state with a funding impasse and insufficient direct support from the existing governance system.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Current treasury withdrawal voting has created perceived competition for scarce resources, hostility, toxicity, and gridlock among governance coalitions.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Treasury governance is pivotal for Cardano's stability and growth and should provide clear direction, coherent strategy, and a specific roadmap.
Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Treasury governance should be explicitly addressed and supported by the governance system and constitution.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The NCL should not be treated as a potential overall budget because it is only a vague initial approximation and cannot replace effective treasury governance.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Cardano should use dedicated balanced budgets with thematic domains rather than voting isolated individual treasury withdrawals against one another.
missing: Independent evidence for: Cardano treasury governance is currently in a poor state with a funding impasse and insufficient direct support from the existing governance system.
Independent evidence for: Current treasury withdrawal voting has created perceived competition for scarce resources, hostility, toxicity, and gridlock among governance coalitions.
Independent evidence for: Treasury governance is pivotal for Cardano's stability and growth and should provide clear direction, coherent strategy, and a specific roadmap.
Independent evidence for: The NCL should not be treated as a potential overall budget because it is only a vague initial approximation and cannot replace effective treasury governance.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Treasury governance should be explicitly addressed and supported by the governance system and constitution." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Cardano treasury governance is currently in a poor state with a funding impasse and insufficient direct support from the existing governance system." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
